description: Per-item review files. Stage 1 writes {type}-{number}.json. Stage 2 writes {type}-{number}.stage2.json. Stage 3 reads both side by side.

confidence.description: How confident you are in your VERDICT specifically. High = you traced the code and are sure. Medium = you checked but there are areas you couldn't fully verify. Low = you're uncertain and flagging for closer review.

scope_estimate.description: Risk surface, not diff size. Small = isolated change, few callers, no state/format implications. Medium = touches shared code or has moderate blast radius. Large = crosses module boundaries, affects state persistence, or could break other language plugins.

stage1_file.description: Written by Stage 1 assessment agent to review/results/{type}-{number}.json

summary.description: What this item does and why this verdict

potential_duplicates.description: PR/issue numbers that MIGHT address the same thing based on title/description. Do NOT claim duplicates — just flag candidates for the orchestrator to verify.

real_problem.description: True if the contributor identified a genuine bug/issue, even if their fix is wrong. Set this on REJECT verdicts where the underlying problem is real.

suggested_fix.description: When real_problem is true but verdict is REJECT: describe what the correct fix would look like. Stage 3 uses this to implement it properly.

stage2_file.description: Written by Stage 2 agent to review/results/{type}-{number}.stage2.json. NEVER modify the Stage 1 file.

role.description: challenger = devil's advocate (for items Stage 1 accepted). advocate = angel's advocate (for items Stage 1 rejected).

counter_case.description: Challengers: the strongest argument AGAINST. Advocates: the strongest argument FOR. Required regardless of your final verdict.

stage3_section.description: Added by Stage 3 adjudicator to the Stage 1 file (review/results/{type}-{number}.json) after making and executing a decision.

decision.description: Stage 3 uses different vocabulary from Stages 1/2 (IMPLEMENT vs ACCEPT) to make the decision layer distinct.

reasoning.description: Why — especially if overriding either stage's verdict

commit.description: Short SHA if implemented, null otherwise

cross_item_file.description: Written by Stage 2 orchestrator to review/results/_cross-item.json after all sub-agents complete.
